cot_prefix: Okay, I need to find the best title for this passage. Let me read through it again and see what the main points are. The passage starts by talking about the London Olympics and how they were considered the "greenest" Games ever. It mentions that previous Olympics caused environmental damage, but London had a different approach with the "One Planet Olympics" concept. The key points discussed are the cleanup of polluted areas, using recycled materials for construction, green building techniques, waste management with rainwater and recycling, using public transport to reduce emissions, and a clean anti-doping record. Looking at the options: A) How to deal with the anti-doping - The passage does mention the anti-doping success, but it's just one part, not the main focus. B) How to use recycled materials - Recycled materials are a big part, but the passage covers more than just that. C) London's green games - This seems to encompass all the environmental efforts discussed, which is the main theme. D) London's public transport - Public transport is mentioned as part of the strategy, but again, it's one aspect. The passage
